elements.screen: Root element of a screen. Defines the top-level area including width, height, and screen-related styles.
elements.view: Main container directly below the screen. Typically used as the layout area for actual widgets.
elements.lv_obj: Generic base widget in LVGL. Can be used as a container, free area, or style holder.
elements.lv_3dtexture: Special widget for 3D texture content. Available in the editor model but not natively supported in XML runtime.
elements.lv_animimg: Animated image widget for frame-based animations.
elements.lv_arc: Circular or arc widget with interactive or static value range.
elements.lv_arclabel: Label displayed along an arc. Present in the model but currently not natively renderable via XML.
elements.lv_bar: Simple bar indicator for value ranges without direct user input.
elements.lv_button: Clickable button container. Text or images are typically added via child widgets.
elements.lv_buttonmatrix: Matrix of button fields for keyboard or selection layouts.
elements.lv_calendar: Calendar widget for month view and date selection.
elements.lv_calendar-header_arrow: Helper element for calendar header with arrow navigation.
elements.lv_calendar-header_dropdown: Helper element for calendar header with dropdown selection.
elements.lv_canvas: Drawing surface for pixel or image operations.
elements.lv_chart: Chart widget for line, bar, or scatter plots.
elements.lv_checkbox: Checkbox with built-in text and boolean state.
elements.lv_dropdown-list: Helper element for dropdown list content.
elements.lv_image: Image widget for resources, logos, or graphics.
elements.lv_imagebutton: Button with image-based states. Currently not natively renderable via XML.
elements.lv_keyboard: On-screen keyboard, typically used with a textarea.
elements.lv_qrcode: QR code widget for encoded text or data.
elements.lv_label: Text display for single or multi-line content.
elements.lv_led: LED-style status indicator. Present in the model but not natively renderable via XML.
elements.lv_line: Line widget for simple graphical connections or markers.
elements.lv_list: List widget for structured entries or menu items.
elements.lv_lottie: Widget for Lottie animations. Present in the model but not natively renderable via XML.
elements.lv_menu: Hierarchical menu widget for complex navigation structures.
elements.lv_msgbox: Dialog or message box for notifications and simple decisions.
elements.lv_scale: Scale widget for axes, ticks, and value display.
elements.lv_scale-section: Section of a scale with its own value range or style.
elements.lv_spangroup: Container for multiple text spans with different formatting.
elements.lv_spangroup-span: Individual text span within a span group.
elements.lv_spinbox: Numeric input field with step-based changes.
elements.lv_spinner: Loading or progress animation in spinner form.
elements.lv_switch: Toggle switch for boolean on/off states.
elements.lv_table: Table widget with rows and columns.
elements.lv_table-cell: Single table cell within a table.
elements.lv_tabview: Container with multiple tabs and corresponding content areas.
elements.lv_tabview-tab_bar: Helper element for the tab bar of a tabview.
elements.lv_tabview-tab: Single tab with its own content area.
elements.lv_tabview-tab_button: Helper element for a tab button.
elements.lv_textarea: Single-line or multi-line text input field.
elements.lv_tileview: Tile-based multi-page view for swipeable areas.
elements.lv_win: Window-like container with title and content areas.
elements.style: Helper element for applying or linking a style.
elements.remove_style: Helper element for removing a specific style.
elements.remove_style_all: Helper element for removing all styles from an element.
elements.bind_style: Helper element for dynamically binding a style.
elements.event_cb: Direct XML helper element for event callbacks with trigger and callback name.
elements.screen_load_event: Helper element for actions when a screen is loaded.
elements.screen_create_event: Helper element for actions when a screen is created.
elements.play_timeline_event: Helper element to start a timeline or animation via event.
elements.subject_toggle_event: Helper element to toggle a subject value via event.
elements.subject_set_int_event: Helper element to set an integer subject via event.
elements.subject_set_float_event: Helper element to set a float subject via event.
elements.subject_set_string_event: Helper element to set a string subject via event.
elements.subject_increment_event: Helper element to increment a subject value via event.
elements.bind_flag_if_eq: Helper element to set a flag if a subject equals a comparison value.
elements.bind_flag_if_not_eq: Helper element to set a flag if a subject does not equal a comparison value.
elements.bind_flag_if_gt: Helper element to set a flag if a subject is greater than a comparison value.
elements.bind_flag_if_ge: Helper element to set a flag if a subject is greater than or equal to a comparison value.
elements.bind_flag_if_lt: Helper element to set a flag if a subject is less than a comparison value.
elements.bind_flag_if_le: Helper element to set a flag if a subject is less than or equal to a comparison value.
elements.bind_state_if_eq: Helper element to set a state if a subject equals a comparison value.
elements.bind_state_if_not_eq: Helper element to set a state if a subject does not equal a comparison value.
elements.bind_state_if_gt: Helper element to set a state if a subject is greater than a comparison value.
elements.bind_state_if_ge: Helper element to set a state if a subject is greater than or equal to a comparison value.
elements.bind_state_if_lt: Helper element to set a state if a subject is less than a comparison value.
elements.bind_state_if_le: Helper element to set a state if a subject is less than or equal to a comparison value.

attributes.name: Sets name.
attributes.subject: Sets subject.
attributes.title: Sets title.
attributes.eventGroup: Functional grouping for shared event dispatchers in the MCU project.
attributes.eventType: Free functional identifier for the event type inside an event group.
attributes.useMessages: Marks that the event callback should be prepared for message-based forwarding.
